# Abendrot

> Abendrot is a free, open-source, native macOS menu-bar app that warms your screen's
> color temperature on every display. It ships a small command-line tool, `abendrot`,
> that lets an AI assistant (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor) or a shell script read the app's
> live state and change its settings — set warmth, switch schedule mode, exclude apps,
> and trigger a momentary true-color reveal.

The `abendrot` CLI is a same-user, local-session, visual-state-only control surface: no
network listener, no socket, no privileged helper. It persists settings to the app's
preference domain and, when the app is running, applies them live and confirms the change.

## Controlling Abendrot (for agents)

- [AGENTS.md](https://abendrot.app/AGENTS.md): The canonical reference for the `abendrot`
  CLI — every command with a real example, every settable preference with its value range,
  the `status --json` field reference, the exit-code table, the trust boundary, and a raw
  `defaults` fallback appendix. Start here to set warmth and read status.

## Quick start

```sh
abendrot set warmth 0.8    # set global warmth (0.0 none … 1.0 warmest)
abendrot status --json     # read live state back and verify the change
```

## About

- [Homepage](https://abendrot.app/): What Abendrot is, who it's for, and how to install it.
